doctor.title: $ doctor — %s

status.create_roadmap: Create a ROADMAP.md with ## Phase and - [x]/- [ ] tasks

onboarding.subtitle: — files and structure
onboarding.instruction: Each file has a role. The scaffold creates, the agent completes.
product.title: $ new
product.readiness.question: Do you know what you want to build?
product.readiness.desc:
Before coding, let's understand the product.
The biggest vibecoding mistake is skipping this step.
product.readiness.clear: I have a clear idea — just want to structure it
product.readiness.refine: I have a direction — need to refine
product.readiness.problem: I have a problem — don't know the solution yet
product.readiness.explore: I want to explore — show me possibilities
product.name: Project name
product.tagline.placeholder: Scaffold that thinks about the product before the code
product.scale.weekend: Weekend vibe — 2-3 days, ship and see if it sticks
product.problem: What pain does this product solve?
product.problem.desc: Be specific. 'Developers waste time on X because Y'
product.audience.general: General public — viral beyond dev bubble
product.hook: The HOOK — why does someone try it the first time?
product.hook.desc:
Should fit in a tweet. If it doesn't, it's too complex.

Examples:
  OK: 'Turns your commits into a pixel art city'
  OK: 'Your AI agent has a pet that dies if you stop coding'
  Bad: 'Development platform with integrated AI'
product.share_trigger: The SHARE TRIGGER — what exact moment makes them share?
product.share_trigger.desc:
Screenshot? Comparison? Absurd result? Achievement?

Be specific about the MOMENT:
  OK: 'When they see their building is bigger than their friend's'
  Bad: 'When they have a good experience'
product.loop: The LOOP — why do they come back tomorrow?
product.loop.desc: What changes between today and tomorrow?
product.mechanics.identity: Identity Visualization — activity becomes visual artifact
product.mechanics.idle: Idle / Incremental — progress while you're away
product.mechanics.streak: Streak + Loss Aversion — daily streak with punishment
product.mechanics.pet: Virtual Pet / Companion — creature that reflects activity
product.mechanics.copresence: Body Doubling / Co-presence — work alongside others
product.monetization.free: Free forever — grow by viral, monetize later
product.monetization.freemium: Freemium — core free, paid premium features
product.monetization.subscription: Subscription — recurring value from the start
product.mom_test.desc:
Explain in 2 sentences to a non-tech person.
If you can't, the concept needs simplification.
product.mom_test.placeholder:
Sentence 1: What it is.
Sentence 2: Why it's cool.
product.explore_instruction: For free exploration, use Vibe Forge:
product.explore_detail2: It researches trends and generates viral concepts.
product.explore_detail3: Coming back with an idea? Run: vs new
commands.unknown:
unknown command: %s
Type /help to see available commands
commands.switch_success:
● Switching to %s...
● Context kept — injecting CONTEXT.md
● %s active
commands.help:
Available commands:
  /switch <agent>    Switch LLM agent (claude, codex, gemini, ollama, aider)
  /doctor             Project health check
  /status             Show context and roadmap
  /context            Show loaded context files
  /clear              Clear visual history
  /exit               Exit VibeForge
  /help               Show this help
scaffold.principle.tdd: Test-Driven Development — write tests before code
scaffold.principle.event-sourcing: Event Sourcing — event history as source of truth
scaffold.workflow: Task -> read files -> implement -> build -> commit -> push
scaffold.section.author: ## Author
onboarding.file.product_brief.desc: Complete product brief — hook, loop, share trigger, viral loop, monetization
onboarding.file.product_brief.scaffold.1: Hook: why someone tries it the first time
onboarding.file.product_brief.scaffold.3: Share Trigger: exact moment that generates sharing
onboarding.file.product_brief.agent.1: Mechanic refinement after validation with real users
onboarding.file.product_brief.agent.2: Loop update based on real retention metrics
onboarding.file.persona.desc: Product personas — who uses, how, and why
onboarding.file.persona.scaffold.1: Primary audience with behaviors and motivations
onboarding.file.persona.agent.1: Personas refined after interviews with real users
onboarding.file.viral_loop.desc: Diagram and analysis of the product's viral loop
onboarding.file.viral_loop.scaffold.1: ASCII diagram of the viral loop
onboarding.file.readme.desc: Project entry point — first file read by anyone or any agent
onboarding.file.readme.scaffold.5: Useful commands (make up, make db, etc)
onboarding.file.readme.agent.1: CI, coverage and version badges as configured
onboarding.file.readme.agent.2: Screenshots and demos when the project has interface
onboarding.file.changelog.desc: Version history following Keep a Changelog + Semantic Versioning
onboarding.file.changelog.scaffold.1: Unreleased section with initial scaffold items
onboarding.file.changelog.agent.1: Entry on each release with what was added
onboarding.file.license.scaffold.1: Proprietary or MIT license based on wizard choice
onboarding.file.license.scaffold.2: Copyright with author name and year
onboarding.file.editorconfig.desc: Indentation and encoding config for all editors and agents
onboarding.file.editorconfig.scaffold.1: Global rules: LF, UTF-8, trim whitespace
onboarding.file.editorconfig.scaffold.2: Per-language rules: Go (tabs), TS/JS (2 spaces), Python/C++ (4 spaces)
onboarding.file.editorconfig.agent.1: Additional rules as new languages are added
onboarding.file.claude_md.desc: Main agent instructions — automatically read by Claude Code
onboarding.file.claude_md.scaffold.1: Absolute rules (never commit without tests, always push, etc)
onboarding.file.claude_md.scaffold.2: Active principles (TDD, Clean Arch, SOLID — based on your choice)
onboarding.file.claude_md.scaffold.3: Standard workflow (read → implement → test → commit → push)
onboarding.file.claude_md.agent.1: Specific technical decisions discovered during development
onboarding.file.claude_md.agent.2: Codebase patterns the agent should follow
onboarding.file.settings.scaffold.1: List of allowed commands (git, make, docker, npm...)
onboarding.file.settings.agent.1: Additional permissions as the project needs
onboarding.file.hooks.desc: Runs before each agent tool call
onboarding.file.context.desc: Complete project briefing — context injected in each agent session
onboarding.file.context.agent.2: Integrations implemented and how they work
onboarding.file.roadmap.scaffold.1: Phase structure (Phase 0 Setup, Phase 1 MVP, etc)
onboarding.file.roadmap.agent.1: Specific items per phase based on real project
onboarding.file.roadmap.agent.2: Status updates as features are implemented
onboarding.file.architecture.scaffold.1: Empty sections with headers (Overview, Diagram, Flows)
onboarding.file.architecture.agent.1: ASCII architecture diagram after first implementations
onboarding.file.glossary.desc: Glossary of technical and domain terms
onboarding.file.glossary.scaffold.1: File structure with sections by letter
onboarding.file.glossary.scaffold.2: Technical terms from the chosen stack
onboarding.file.glossary.agent.2: Acronyms and abbreviations used in the codebase
onboarding.file.testing.scaffold.3: Commands to run each test type
onboarding.file.testing.scaffold.4: Test runner based on chosen stack
onboarding.file.testing.agent.3: Load and performance tests when implemented
onboarding.file.srs.agent.1: Functional requirements based on what was implemented
onboarding.file.srs.agent.3: User stories derived from real features
onboarding.file.rf.desc: Detailed Functional Requirements — what the system must do
onboarding.file.rf.scaffold.1: Standard template per requirement (priority, status, description, input, output)
onboarding.file.rf.scaffold.2: RF-001 placeholder for the first requirement
onboarding.file.rf.agent.1: Complete functional requirements based on implemented features
onboarding.file.rnf.desc: Non-Functional Requirements — how the system operates
onboarding.file.rnf.agent.1: Real metrics based on load tests
onboarding.file.user_stories.desc: User stories from the end user perspective
onboarding.file.user_stories.scaffold.1: Template: As [user], I want [action], so that [benefit]
onboarding.file.user_stories.agent.1: Complete user stories based on implemented features
onboarding.file.use_cases.desc: Use cases — interactions between actors and the system
onboarding.file.use_cases.agent.1: UC-001 to UC-N complete as features implemented
onboarding.file.adr.desc: Architecture Decision Record — tech stack decision
onboarding.file.adr.agent.1: Detailed context of why the choice was made
onboarding.file.adr.agent.2: Alternatives considered and why they were discarded
onboarding.file.adr.agent.3: Consequences and trade-offs of the decision
onboarding.file.ci.desc: CI/CD — runs on each push and PR
onboarding.file.ci.agent.1: Specific steps as the project grows
onboarding.file.release.desc: Automatic release when v*.*.* tag is created
onboarding.file.release.scaffold.2: Automatic release notes generation from CHANGELOG
onboarding.file.dependabot.scaffold.1: Weekly schedule for Go modules, npm, GitHub Actions
onboarding.file.pr_template.desc: Standard template for all project PRs
onboarding.file.pr_template.scaffold.4: Checklist: CI green, tests, CHANGELOG, no secrets
onboarding.file.pr_template.agent.1: Project-specific checklist items as it evolves
onboarding.file.precommit.desc: Hooks that run before each commit ensuring quality
onboarding.file.precommit.scaffold.3: Automatic lint of the main language
onboarding.file.security.desc: Security policy and how to report vulnerabilities
onboarding.file.security.agent.1: Detailed scope based on implemented features
onboarding.file.setup.desc: Environment setup from scratch in one command
